articles.title: Half-time whistle: Airbus hits its stride on road to annual delivery goal
articles.summary: Airbus confirmed 89 aircraft deliveries in June 2026 to 49 customers — its strongest monthly total of the year — lifting its H1 2026 tally to 351 jets, a 15% gain over the first six months of 2025; Airbus now targets 870 deliveries officially for 2026, with an internal goal exceeding 900 for the first time.
articles.novelis_angle: A 15% YoY lift in Airbus H1 deliveries signals sustained structural aluminium plate demand for Novelis's Koblenz aerospace facility, which supplies plate for Airbus narrow- and wide-body programmes.

articles.title: Hydro enables Norway's first closed-loop aluminium façade in Oslo refurbishment
articles.summary: Hydro's WICONA building systems brand closed the loop on the 21,000 m² Sørkedalsveien 6 office building in Oslo: the existing aluminium façade was dismantled, sent to Stena Recycling in Sweden, converted into Hydro CIRCAL 75R (≥75% post-consumer scrap), and re-installed as the new façade — marking Norway's first closed-loop aluminium building refurbishment.
articles.novelis_angle: European B&C specifiers are beginning to mandate documented closed-loop provenance for façade systems; Novelis's pre-painted FRP products such as Super Windy® face a competitive landscape where circularity credentials from scrap-to-façade are becoming a differentiating factor.

articles.title: Aluminium prices climb as LME stockpiles slip under 300,000 tonnes for first time since 2022
articles.summary: LME aluminium warehouse stocks fell to 295,550 t on 7 July 2026, breaching the 300,000 t threshold for the first time since 2022 following a ~19,225 t drawdown over five weeks; the tightening in prompt availability pushed the three-month contract up 0.4% to $3,103/t.
